Consolidate to a single implementation, remove dead code, and add a small shell test to lock behavior.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:08:53.010083-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (generated category flag logic consolidated)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-59t4","title":"acfs-update: refresh PATH after supabase install","description":"acfs-update installs Supabase CLI into ~/.local/bin, which may be created during the install. Ensure PATH is refreshed before version capture so the new binary is discoverable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T02:08:13.590188-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T02:08:25.841273-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T02:08:25.841273-05:00","close_reason":"Refresh PATH after Supabase install before version capture."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-59vr","title":"Enhance bv --robot-insights output fields in beads-lesson.tsx","description":"BACKGROUND: The beads-lesson.tsx shows output fields for 'bv --robot-insights' as ['PageRank', 'betweenness', 'HITS', 'cycles', 'k-core']. However, per the BV README (which I read in full from /tmp/dicklesworthstone_tools/bv/README.md), the command returns additional metrics. CURRENT STATE: Line 177 shows: output={['PageRank', 'betweenness', 'HITS', 'cycles', 'k-core']} FULL METRICS PER README: PageRank, betweenness, HITS (hubs/authorities), eigenvector, critical_path, cycles, k-core, articulation_points, slack RECOMMENDATION: Add at minimum 'eigenvector' and 'critical_path' to give users a more complete picture of the available graph analytics. PRIORITY: Low (P3) - This is an enhancement, not a bug. The current list is accurate but incomplete. OVERARCHING GOAL: Help users understand the full power of BV's graph analysis capabilities so they can make informed decisions about which metrics to use for task prioritization.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T17:51:09.635902-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T17:52:35.283729-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T17:52:35.283729-05:00","close_reason":"Added eigenvector and critical_path to bv --robot-insights output fields for more complete representation of available metrics","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-59vr","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9c80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T17:51:35.754103-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5amx","title":"Deep audit: cross-commit agent code review","description":"Review code across the repo (not just recent commits) for bugs/security/reliability issues; focus on installer scripts, generator, and web; fix any high-confidence issues and push.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:28:20.174987-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T15:28:29.567082-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5amx","title":"Deep audit: cross-commit agent code review","description":"Review code across the repo (not just recent commits) for bugs/security/reliability issues; focus on installer scripts, generator, and web; fix any high-confidence issues and push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:28:20.174987-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T15:48:04.410505-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T15:48:04.410505-05:00","close_reason":"Completed repo-wide audit; fixed gh auth stderr capture in onboard auth checks and hardened installer phase label parsing. Follow-up filed: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-sa4e."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gn2","title":"Fix Ubuntu upgrade resume pre-upgrade reboot stage + target-version override","description":"Installer Ubuntu auto-upgrade resume path bug: install.sh wrote wrong state key for reboot-required (".ubuntu_upgrade.stage" instead of ".ubuntu_upgrade.current_stage") and didn’t persist enabled/target_version/original_version, so resume could mis-detect stage and upgrade_resume.sh could crash when upgrade_path missing. Also upgrade_resume.sh + ubuntu_upgrade.sh hardcoded 25.10, ignoring --target-ubuntu/state target_version. Land ChartreuseCastle fix (commit 2a7659b) across install.sh, scripts/lib/upgrade_resume.sh, scripts/lib/ubuntu_upgrade.sh.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T02:02:41.200926-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:05:47.33651-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:05:47.33651-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: fix ubuntu upgrade resume pre-upgrade reboot stage + honor target_version (commit bdc48e0)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","title":"Task: Create command reference page","description":"# Create Command Reference Page ## Parent Feature Reference Documentation (1y8) ## Goal Create a searchable command reference page at `/learn/commands` that lists all commands installed by ACFS. ## Design ### Page Layout ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Command Reference │ │ │ │ [🔍 Search commands...] │ │ │ │ Category: [All] [Agents] [Search] [Git] [System] │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cc [Copy] │ │ │ │ Claude Code - AI coding assistant │ │ │ │ Example: cc "fix the bug in auth.ts" │ │ │ │ [Full docs →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cod [Copy] │ │ │ │ Codex CLI - OpenAI coding agent │ │ │ │ ... │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ... │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Command Data Structure ```typescript // lib/commands.ts interface Command { name: string; fullName: string; description: string; category: 'agents' | 'search' | 'git' | 'system' | 'stack'; example: string; docsUrl?: string; aliases?: string[]; } export const COMMANDS: Command[] = [ // Agents { name: 'cc', fullName: 'Claude Code', description: 'AI coding assistant', category: 'agents', example: 'cc "fix the bug"' }, { name: 'cod', fullName: 'Codex CLI', description: 'OpenAI coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'cod "add tests"' }, { name: 'gmi', fullName: 'Gemini CLI', description: 'Google coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'gmi "review code"' }, // Search { name: 'rg', fullName: 'ripgrep', description: 'Ultra-fast code search', category: 'search', example: 'rg "pattern" ./src' }, { name: 'fd', fullName: 'fd-find', description: 'Fast file finder', category: 'search', example: 'fd "*.ts"' }, { name: 'fzf', fullName: 'Fuzzy Finder', description: 'Interactive fuzzy search', category: 'search', example: 'fzf' }, // Git { name: 'lazygit', fullName: 'LazyGit', description: 'Visual git interface', category: 'git', example: 'lazygit' }, { name: 'git', fullName: 'Git', description: 'Version control', category: 'git', example: 'git status' }, // System { name: 'ntm', fullName: 'Named Tmux Manager', description: 'Session management', category: 'system', example: 'ntm new project' }, { name: 'lsd', fullName: 'LSDeluxe', description: 'Modern ls replacement', category: 'system', example: 'lsd -la' }, { name: 'bat', fullName: 'Bat', description: 'Cat with syntax highlighting', category: 'system', example: 'bat file.ts' }, { name: 'zoxide', fullName: 'Zoxide', description: 'Smart cd replacement', category: 'system', example: 'z project' }, // Stack { name: 'bd', fullName: 'Beads CLI', description: 'Task graph management', category: 'stack', example: 'bd list' }, { name: 'bv', fullName: 'Beads Viewer', description: 'Kanban task viewer', category: 'stack', example: 'bv' }, { name: 'ubs', fullName: 'Ultimate Bug Scanner', description: 'Code quality scanner', category: 'stack', example: 'ubs .' }, { name: 'cass', fullName: 'CASS', description: 'Session search', category: 'stack', example: 'cass' }, { name: 'cm', fullName: 'CASS Memory', description: 'Procedural memory', category: 'stack', example: 'cm context "auth"' }, { name: 'caam', fullName: 'CAAM', description: 'Account manager', category: 'stack', example: 'caam status' }, { name: 'am', fullName: 'Agent Mail', description: 'Agent coordination', category: 'stack', example: 'am status' }, ]; ``` ### Features 1. **Search** - Filter by command name or description 2. **Category tabs** - Quick filter by category 3. **Copy button** - One-click copy command 4. **Expandable** - Show full usage on click 5. **Deep links** - `/learn/commands#cc` jumps to command ### SEO - Title: "ACFS Command Reference" - Description: "Quick reference for all commands installed by Agent Flywheel" - Keywords: individual command names ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page accessible at `/learn/commands` - [ ] All ACFS commands listed - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Category tabs work - [ ] Copy button copies command - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/lib/commands.ts` - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/commands/page.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/command-ref-card.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:22:20.811137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented /learn/commands with search, category filters, deep links, and copyable examples.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1y8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.574895-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","title":"Add SSH key verification step (check files exist)","description":"# Task: Add SSH Key Verification Step ## Parent Epic [EPIC] SSH Key Generation Clarity ## Problem After running ssh-keygen, users don't know how to verify it worked or understand what was created. They have two files (public/private) but don't know which is which or what they're for. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Add verification section after the command: \`\`\`tsx

You should see two files:

  • acfs_ed25519 — Your private key (keep this secret!)
  • acfs_ed25519.pub — Your public key (this gets shared)
 Think of it like a mailbox: the public key is your address (you share it), the private key is your mailbox key (only you have it). \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows verification command - [ ] Explains the two files and their purposes - [ ] Uses relatable analogy (mailbox) - [ ] Reassures user about what to keep secret ## Why This Matters Users who don't verify their key might not realize when something went wrong. The public/private distinction prevents confusion later when they need to share their public key.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:21.115008-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:21.115008-05:00","close_reason":"Added 'Verify Your Key Was Created' section with ls command to check both key files exist, explanation of private vs public key, and mailbox analogy","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-unqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:12.253977-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
